NEWS ASSOCldtl


CALDWELL, Idaho, March 12.-
Meyer, Haywood and Pettibone
were brought here this morning for
the argument on their attorneys
motion to dismiss because the case
was not tried at terms of court fol-
lowing the indictment, as required
by law. The prosecution will show
it was anxious for trial but was
prevented by pending appeals of
the defendants.


A vigorous contest will come
over the pending motion for a
change of venue from this to Can
yon county on the ground OF
prejudice.


When court opened this morning
Judge Bryan made an order calling
Judge Wood, Of Boise, to preside.
The motions came up at 2 o'clock.
The prosecution will call the
courts attention to the fact that
the mandate of the United States
supreme cour is not here, but will
be delivered the last of the week,
and will ask that hearing of mo-
lions be set for Monday next.
